data.title: Trump administration said to be preparing executive order targeting 'woke' AI
data.description:
The White House is preparing an executive order that would target what it deems to be "woke" artificial intelligence models, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

The order would require AI models of companies with federal contracts to be politically neutral, amid concerns that AI models may have liberal bias.

AI models, trained using information from the internet and other published sources, sometimes generate answers that are factually incorrect or misleading - a phenomenon called "AI hallucination."

For example, Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) came under fire last year as its AI assistant Gemini showed a black George Washington and racially diverse Nazis.

This trend has troubled White House officials, including AI Czar David Sacks - one of the architects of the executive order targeting "woke" AI. But the order may be controversial, given concerns in the tech industry that the government could play favorites. 

xAI - owned by Trump's on-again, off-again ally Elon Musk - prioritizes being "anti-woke," but recently faced criticism after its Grok chatbot generated antisemitic content.

Dario Amodei, CEO of Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN)-backed Anthropic, recently criticized a Republican proposal to bar states from regulating AI for 10 years. Anthropic has also hired former Biden administration staffers.

To note, OpenAI, Google (NASDAQ:GOOG), Anthropic, and xAI this week secured contracts worth up to $200M from the Pentagon to expand the federal use of their AI tools.

The Trump administration is expected to unveil several executive orders next week, including the one on "woke" AI, aimed at ensuring the U.S. maintains its AI edge over China.

The orders would aim to promote exports of American chips and AI tools, and accelerate permitting for data centers, sources told WSJ.

data.title: Telia Company AB (publ) reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Telia Company AB (publ) press release (OTCPK:TLSNF): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.50.
  • Revenue of $19.8B (-2.0% Y/Y).
  • Service revenue like for like increased 1.0% driven by continued strong momentum in Sweden and the Baltics. –
  • Adjusted EBITDA like for like increased 6.2% driven by service revenue growth and lower operational expenses across most markets as a result from the Change program implemented in the fourth quarter of 2024.

data.title: Crypto market cap surpasses $4 trillion amid passage of U.S. stablecoin bill
data.description:
The total market capitalization of cryptoassets surpassed $4 trillion for the first time, fueled by a surge in altcoins and growing momentum from a broad U.S. legislative effort to regulate the industry.

The milestone followed the passage of the first-ever federal legislation for stablecoins, a key accomplishment that lawmakers have dubbed “Crypto Week.”

According to Coingecko data, the entire crypto market cap stood at around $4.003 trillion, with over $260.2 billion worth of crypto traded in the past 24 hours.

The $4 trillion record also comes amid a broader rally in the crypto market. Bitcoin reached $120,134—bringing it within striking distance of its all-time high near $123,000, and Ether jumped as much as 6% to $3,628.

The bill, supported by Republicans and endorsed by President Donald Trump, proposes federal or state oversight of dollar-pegged stablecoins, aiming to legitimize the $265 billion market that Citigroup analysts estimate could expand to $3.7 trillion by 2030.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Nvidia's CEO, China's commerce minister discuss foreign investment, AI - report
data.description:
China's Commerce Minister Wang Wentao told Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang that he hoped companies, including Nvidia, would provide high-quality and reliable products and services to customers in China, Reuters reported, citing a statement from the ministry.

Huang noted that the Chinese market was very attractive, and Nvidia was willing to strengthen cooperation with Chinese partners over AI, the report added, citing the ministry's statement.

Nvidia did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Wang noted that China's policy of attracting foreign investment will not change and the door to openness will only open wider, according to the report.

Huang also met with Ren Hongbin, chairman of China Council for the Promotion of International Trade, and the country's Vice Premier He Lifeng.

Chinese officials told Huang they welcomed foreign companies to carry on investing in the Asian nation, the Nvidia CEO said at a press conference in Beijing on Wednesday, the report noted.

Huang had praised AI models from China’s Deepseek (DEEPSEEK), Alibaba (BABA), and Tencent (OTCPK:TCEHY) as “world class” at the event, calling AI a revolution in global logistics.

In a separate statement on Friday, China's commerce ministry said that the U.S. had told China that it would approve the sales of Nvidia's H20 AI chips to Chinese customers.

Huang had said on Wednesday that the company will boost supply of Chinese-compliant H20 chips in the coming months and look to bring more advanced semiconductors to the country. He added that demand for H20 chips from Chinese customers was high but no purchase orders have been fulfilled as it was waiting for U.S. approval of export licenses.

Huang's comments had come after the company said it hoped to resume sales of its H20 graphics processing units, or GPUs, to Chinese clients, citing U.S. government assurances that export licenses will be granted. U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said the move was part of negotiations on rare earth minerals.

Huang had also unveiled a new compliant chip, the RTX PRO, aimed at smart factories and logistics.

The sale of the H20 chips had been halted in April despite the chips being designed to bypass export restrictions.

data.title: Sarepta plunges after third patient death tied to gene therapy - report
data.description:
Sarepta Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SRPT) said a third patient has died from acute liver failure after receiving one of its gene therapies, intensifying scrutiny over the safety of its treatments, Bloomberg reported.

The latest death occurred last month during an early-stage trial for a therapy targeting limb-girdle muscular dystrophy. The company told regulators and investigators about the death “in an appropriate and timely manner,” Bloomberg reported, quoting a company spokesperson.

The report follows the recent deaths of two teenage boys who were treated with Elevidys, a gene therapy marketed with Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) for Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD), a rare muscle disorder. Both of those fatalities occurred in patients who were non-ambulatory, or unable to walk because of advanced stages of the disease.

As a result, Sarepta (NASDAQ:SRPT) halted an ongoing clinical trial and temporarily suspended distribution of Elevidys for patients who are no longer able to walk.

The company said Wednesday that it had agreed to warn doctors and patients about the risk of liver failure from Elevidys, per an FDA request. Talks with the FDA are ongoing regarding safety concerns for treating patients who can no longer walk.

Sarepta (SRPT) also announced Wednesday it will cut over a third of its workforce and pause several drug programs, moves expected to save $400 million annually.

Shares fell nearly 16% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Stock index futures gain after S&P 500, Nasdaq notch record closes
data.description:
Stock index futures ticked up on Friday, a day after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq notched record closes, helped by the retail sales report and earnings releases. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.2%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.44%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.91%.

Wall Street closed out trading higher on Thursday, with both the Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 surging to fresh record highs.

“Markets have put in a decent performance over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq both reaching fresh all-time highs, with the global rally mostly continuing this morning. The advance was driven by another batch of positive U.S. data, including higher-than-expected retail sales and then a 5th consecutive weekly decline in initial jobless claims,” Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

With the stronger data and a continued rise in market inflation pricing, investors dialed down the amount of Fed rate cuts expected this year to 43bps, the lowest this has been since February. Reid added. 

“The problem for the Federal Reserve and markets is that the U.S. administration’s policy continues to create considerable uncertainty about the economic outlook,” UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

It is a light day for economic releases, with mainly the June Housing Starts and Permits report and July consumer sentiment data. 

Starts are expected to edge up to 1.30 million unit rate in June from a sluggish 1.256 million in May. 

Sentiment is forecasted up slightly to 61.4 in the first reading for July from 60.7 in the June final.

“Political partisanship will be delayed in the Michigan consumer sentiment data (Republicans live in the best of all possible worlds, Democrats live in the worst of times—according to survey evidence),” Donovan said. 

On the earnings end, 3M Company (MMM), American Express (AXP), Charles Schwab (SCHW) will release their results.

data.title: Europe markets in green as investors focus on corporate earnings
data.description:
London (UKX:IND) +0.20% to 8,990, supported by strength in energy, mining, and retail stocks.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.32% to 24,448. Producer prices in Germany declined by 1.3% Y/Y in June.

France: (CAC:IND) +0.48% to 7859.

Slovakia's current account deficit shrank to €77.0 million in May.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.33% higher to 548.86 on Friday, as investors closely monitored corporate earnings while still staying on the edge for prospects of a trade deal with the U.S. Market optimism was fueled by stronger-than-expected U.S. retail sales in June. 

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 2 basis points to 4.44%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 2.69%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 4.67%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: NFLX, NSC, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher Friday morning after the S&P 500 closed at a record high, buoyed by strong corporate earnings and upbeat U.S. economic data. 

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Interactive Brokers (NASDAQ:IBKR) +5% — Shares rose after Q2 results topped expectations, fueled by surging trading activity. Daily average revenue trades hit 3.55M, up 49% Y/Y. Commission revenue reached $516M (+27% Y/Y), while net interest income rose to $860M from $770M in Q1. Trading volume increased across stocks (+31%), options (+24%), and futures (+18%). Customer accounts grew to 3.87M, up 32% Y/Y.
  • Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) +5% — Shares climbed following a report that Union Pacific (UNP) is in early talks to acquire its smaller rival. The deal would create the only coast-to-coast rail network in the U.S., combining UNP’s western routes with NSC’s 19,500-mile system across 22 eastern states. While UNP CEO Jim Vena has touted the benefits of a transcontinental railroad, any deal would face major regulatory hurdles from the Surface Transportation Board, DOJ, labor unions, and Amtrak.

Biggest stock losers

  • Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) -2% — Shares dipped despite a Q2 beat and raised full-year revenue guidance. Revenue rose 16% Y/Y to $11.08B, topping expectations, while operating income surged to $3.78B from $2.6B a year ago. Operating margin improved to 34.1% (vs. 27.2% Y/Y). Net income climbed 46% to $3.13B. Netflix now sees FY revenue of $44.8B–$45.2B, up from a prior view of $43.5B–$44.5B (vs. $44.74B est.), and lifted its currency-neutral operating margin forecast to 29.5%, up from 29% (30% on an as-reported basis).

More on related stocks:

  • Netflix, Inc. (NFLX) Q2 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
  • Interactive Brokers Group, Inc. (IBKR) Q2 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
  • Netflix Earnings Preview: Margin Guidance Continues To Be Revised Higher
  • Netflix lifts 2025 revenue guidance to $45.2B while advancing global ad tech and live content
  • Union Pacific exploring deal to buy Norfolk Southern - WSJ

data.title: Meta recruits two key Apple AI experts after poaching their boss - report
data.description:
• Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) has reportedly brought on two prominent artificial intelligence researchers who formerly worked at Apple (AAPL), following the recent hiring of their former supervisor from the iPhone maker.
  • The social networking giant hired Mark Lee and Tom Gunter for its Superintelligence Labs team, Bloomberg reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.
  • Lee has started at Meta after leaving Apple in recent days, while Gunter will begin work in the near future, said the people, who asked not to be identified because the hires haven’t been announced.
  • The departures mark the latest high-profile AI hire by Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg, who is building out the company’s new AI superintelligence division.

data.title: American Tungsten upsizes private placement to $7.0M
data.description:
American Tungsten (OTCQB:DEMRF) has announced an increase in the maximum size of its previously announced non-brokered private placement, the company will now offer up to 14,000,000 common shares at a price of $0.50 per share, aiming to raise aggregate gross proceeds of up to $7.0 million.

This is an increase from the previously stated maximum of 5,000,000 shares.

For this offering, American Tungsten will pay finders' fees of up to 7.0% of the gross proceeds from shares sold through eligible finders.

Additionally, these finders will receive non-transferable warrants equal to 7.0% of the shares they placed. Each warrant will allow the holder to buy one share at $0.50 for up to 24 months from its issue date.

The company will use the net proceeds from the offering to fund exploration work programs, option payments, mineral property acquisitions, marketing and for general working capital purposes.

The offering is not subject to a minimum aggregate amount of subscriptions. 

All securities issued in connection with the offering will be subject to a statutory hold period of four months plus a day from the date of issuance.
